steps.title: Identify what the claim requires
steps.content: The passage says the speaker "declares her intention to create art that has a universal appeal across generations." The correct quotation must show the speaker actively stating a plan to make something for people of all ages — not simply describing a need or addressing an unrelated subject.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
Work through each option:
  • Choice A — "Our world, so worn and weary, / Needs music, pure and strong.": Describes society's need for music but contains no declaration by the speaker of her own artistic intention. ✗
  • Choice B — "Let me make the songs for the people, / Songs for the old and young.": The speaker directly declares her intention ("Let me make") to create songs for both old and young — universal, cross-generational appeal stated in her own voice. ✓
  • Choice C — "God bless our native land, / Land of the newly free.": A patriotic address with no reference to artistic creation or generational scope. ✗
  • Choice D — "My mother's kiss, my mother's kiss, / I feel its impress now.": A personal memory with no connection to universal artistic intent. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
